rows.notes: Page title at top centre. Clear, legible handwriting; no amounts on this header.
Clear left-side entry. Pounds/shillings/pence columns well aligned. Pence shows '1' with a trailing '3/4' (threequarters) symbol; digits are distinct.

Handwriting heavily corrected/overwritten. I see an initial '156' beneath heavier ink that looks like '570' struck through/overwritten; chosen conservative reading '156-0-0'. Column alignment is consistent with other left-side rows but the pounds figure is uncertain due to an overwritten correction.
Left-side entry. Pounds '101', shillings '8' and pence '4' are all clearly formed and aligned in the columns. Small ink blotting but digits unambiguous.
Left-side entry. Pounds '1094', shillings '12' and pence '7' are clear. There is a small loop after the pence digit interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) → 0.5; loop is distinct.

Left-side entry. Pounds/shillings readable as 278-8; pence reads '9' with a small loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) giving .5. Slight flourish on fraction but consistent with other halfpenny marks on page.
Left-side subtotal line. Numerals are well formed and aligned; pence shows '10' with a clear '3/4' (threequarters) mark. Matches the centred 'Ex receptis' total shown below.
Centre-right heading introducing payments/expenditure column. Word is legible and positioned above the right-side amounts.
Right-side entry. Pounds '149', shillings '11' and pence '8' are distinct and aligned in right-side £/s/d columns. No obvious fraction mark after pence.

Right-side entry. Pounds and shillings read clearly as 799-7. Pence appears as '8' with a small trailing loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny). The fraction loop is slightly faint/blurred which reduces confidence.
Right-side entry. Pounds/shillings read as 274-12. Pence shows '2' with a following '3/4' style mark (threequarters) though the fraction mark is slightly compressed; read as 0.75.
Right-side subtotal. Clear alignment and legible numerals: 1223-11-6 with a distinct '3/4' (threequarters) fraction mark after the pence.
Centered summary line restating total receipts. Numerals identical and well aligned with left subtotal; pence shows clear '10' and '3/4' fraction mark.
Centered deduction line mirroring right-side subtotal. Clear figures and fraction '3/4' after pence; alignment confirms reading.
Centered resulting balance. Pounds '675', shillings '18' and pence '4' are clear and in valid ranges. No fraction mark visible after the pence.

rows.description: Computus comprobatus per nos hoc Nov: 2d. 1789.
rows.notes: Centered verification line (no amounts). Handwriting legible; introduces the signatures below.
Signature line. Name and title legible; no monetary values.
Signature line. Handwriting legible; read as 'H. Richards' with title 'Subrectorem'.
Signature line. Surname and postnominal abbreviation a little stylized ('Burr. Jun.' read as written), but overall legible; no amounts.
Signature line. 'AM' initials are clear; no monetary values.
Final signature. Handwriting slightly compressed ('deMarchant' combined) but legible; no amounts present.
